skill.name: verging-ai-tts
skill.description: Convert text into speech audio using OpenAI TTS-1-HD through the verging.ai API. Supports six voices, speed control, and MP3 delivery.
skill.summary: Generate speech audio from text through the verging.ai TTS API.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples and TTS documentation text. The confirmed risks are intentional network requests to verging.ai and API-key handling for authentication, so users should review data sharing and secret-management practices before use.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Request for TTS Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to send text input to the verging.ai TTS API and receive a hosted audio URL. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it means user-provided text leaves the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and response URL are explicitly documented. The request is legitimate for TTS generation, but network transfer of user text is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Required
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs users to create and provide a verging.ai API key through an environment variable or Authorization header. This is normal API authentication, but mishandling the key could expose credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly document API-key use. I did not find evidence that the skill exfiltrates keys beyond the intended Authorization header.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are caused by Markdown code fences and inline literals in documentation. No executable Ruby code or dynamic shell invocation was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation blocks or inline formatting. There is no script file or runtime execution path in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches appear to be triggered by text such as TTS model names, parameter tables, and error documentation. The system reconnaissance match is an HTTP status table entry, not host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are descriptive documentation, not cryptographic implementation or system probing code. I did not find evidence of hashing, encryption, or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Critical Obfuscation Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined documentation examples for network use, API-key authentication, and shell snippets into a critical heuristic. The skill contains no hidden code, encoded payload, prompt injection text, or malicious instruction to override review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination is explained by normal setup and API examples. No evidence found for obfuscation, credential theft, or prompt injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Call With Credential Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented usage sends an Authorization header to a verging.ai endpoint. This is necessary for the skill, but it is the main pattern users should review before adoption.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API endpoint and Authorization header are shown directly in the documentation. The use appears intended, not covert.

content.user_title: Generate AI Speech with verging.ai TTS
content.value_statement: Teams need quick speech audio without building a voice pipeline. This skill shows Claude, Codex, and Claude Code how to call verging.ai TTS with voice and speed options.
actual_capabilities: Convert text up to 4096 characters into speech audio.
Use six documented voices: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, and shimmer.
Call the verging.ai TTS API with API-key authentication.
Return an audio URL and credits consumed after generation.
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account, API key, and available credits.
Sends submitted text to the verging.ai service for processing.
The documentation focuses on API usage, not local audio editing.
The README documents MP3 output, while the skill metadata mentions broader formats.

use_cases.title: Create Product Narration
use_cases.description: Generate short voice clips for onboarding flows, demos, or product walkthroughs.

use_cases.title: Draft Learning Audio
use_cases.description: Turn lesson text into speech for review, accessibility checks, or prototype courses.

use_cases.title: Produce Support Prompts
use_cases.description: Create spoken versions of support scripts for testing IVR flows or help content.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Short Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one audio file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tts skill to generate speech for this text with the nova voice at normal speed: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Voices
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose a voice for a short announcement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create two TTS versions of this announcement, one with alloy and one with shimmer. Summarize which voice sounds better for a calm update.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Delivery Speed
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate speech for this training paragraph with the echo voice. Use a slower speed suitable for careful listening.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Batch Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these script sections, keep each under 4096 characters, recommend voices, and prepare TTS requests with consistent speed settings.

output_examples.input: Generate a friendly welcome message using nova.
output_examples.output: A short MP3 speech file is created with a friendly voice, plus a hosted audio link and credit usage summary.

output_examples.input: Create a slower narration for a training paragraph.
output_examples.output: The paragraph is converted into speech with reduced playback speed for clearer listening.

output_examples.input: Compare alloy and shimmer for a support greeting.
output_examples.output: Two audio versions are generated, and the response explains which voice better fits the support tone.

best_practices: Keep source text concise and below the documented 4096 character limit.
Use environment variables or secret storage for the verging.ai API key.
Avoid submitting confidential or regulated text unless your policy allows third-party processing.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys directly into shared prompts, tickets, or chat logs.
Do not submit sensitive customer data without approval for external processing.
Do not expect the skill to edit, mix, or master generated audio.

faq.question: What service does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the verging.ai API, which proxies OpenAI TTS-1-HD for speech generation.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation requires a verging.ai API key in the Authorization header.

faq.question: Which voices are documented?
faq.answer: The documented voices are alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, and shimmer.

faq.question: Can I control speech speed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents speed values from 0.25x to 4.0x.

faq.question: Does this run fully offline?
faq.answer: No. It sends text to the verging.ai API and receives a hosted audio URL.

faq.question: Is it safe for private text?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review your data policy before sending confidential text to a third-party TTS service.
